2606.29026.title: Preventing Error Propagation in Multi-Agent AI through Runtime Monitoring
2606.29026.paper_summary: Multi-agent AI systems can improve answer selection by allowing different language models to exchange reasoning traces, revise initial predictions, and support a final decision. However, such communication may also introduce reliability risks: reasoning from one agent can correct another agent's mistake, but it can also mislead an agent that was initially correct. This paper studies reliable multi-agent AI communication through reasoning exchange and runtime answer revision. We develop a framework in which agents first answer multiple-choice questions independently, then share reasoning traces and revise their decisions. We conduct numerical experiments where we evaluate whether this process improves accuracy, produces more positive than negative answer transitions, and remains effective across domains such as cybersecurity, networking, and general knowledge. The results help identify when multi-agent reasoning improves reliability and when it may propagate errors.
2606.29026.paper_summary_zh: 多代理人工智慧系統可以透過讓不同的語言模型交換推理痕跡、修正初始預測並支持最終決策來改善答案選擇。<lb/>然而，這種通信也可能引入可靠性風險：一個代理的推理可以糾正另一個代理的錯誤，但也可能誤導一個最初是正確的代理。<lb/>本文研究了通過推理交換和運行時答案修正來實現可靠的多代理人工智慧通信。<lb/>我們開發了一個框架，其中代理首先獨立回答多選題，然後分享推理痕跡並修正他們的決策。<lb/>我們進行了數值實驗，以評估這一過程是否提高了準確性，產生的正向答案轉換是否多於負向，並且在網絡安全、網絡和一般知識等領域中是否仍然有效。<lb/>結果有助於識別多代理推理何時改善可靠性以及何時可能傳播錯誤。
2606.29026.author: Shahnewaz Karim Sakib et.al.
2606.29026.authors: Shahnewaz Karim Sakib, Anindya Bijoy Das
